articles.title: What MCP and Integrations Change for Teams Shipping with Coding Agents
articles.description: A practical guide to using Playwright’s MCP tool so coding agents can safely poke, click, and test your real web app instead of hallucinating it.

scene_briefs.headline: Developer at Desk Using MCP-Enabled Browser
scene_briefs.scene: A developer sits at a desk with a single monitor displaying a real web app running in a browser, alongside a terminal window showing Playwright MCP commands being executed, highlighting live interaction with the app.
scene_briefs.article_grounding: Reflects how MCP allows coding agents to interact with real web apps through browsers, moving beyond sandboxed code editing.

scene_briefs.headline: Code Editor Showing Agent’s MCP Calls
scene_briefs.scene: Close-up of a developer’s monitor displaying a code editor with a script where a coding agent’s code includes explicit MCP tool calls to open browsers, click UI elements, and run assertions, with inline comments explaining each step.
scene_briefs.article_grounding: Illustrates the new artifact type—code enhanced with MCP calls—that enables agents to control real systems as described in the article.

scene_briefs.headline: Developer Reviewing Test Results from MCP Interaction
scene_briefs.scene: A developer at their desk reviewing a dashboard on their monitor showing test results and logs from Playwright MCP-driven UI tests, with a terminal window open displaying agent feedback and a checklist of integration steps completed.
scene_briefs.article_grounding: Captures the workflow moment of implementing MCP without breaking existing processes, focusing on validation and integration feedback.

scene_brief.headline: Developer at Desk Using MCP-Enabled Browser
scene_brief.scene: A developer sits at a desk with a single monitor displaying a real web app running in a browser, alongside a terminal window showing Playwright MCP commands being executed, highlighting live interaction with the app.
scene_brief.article_grounding: Reflects how MCP allows coding agents to interact with real web apps through browsers, moving beyond sandboxed code editing.

comparison_report.style_consistency_goal: Keep canonical style DNA identical while varying scene content.

distinctness_notes.rationale: Distinct by context (workspace) and scene intent: A developer sits at a desk with a single monitor displaying a real web app running in a browser, alongside a terminal window showing Playwright MCP commands being executed, highlighting live interaction with the app.

articles.title: What Multi‑Agent Orchestration Changes for Teams Shipping With Coding Agents
articles.description: A practical look at how an "Opus 4.6" style orchestrator could coordinate a fleet of "Codex 5.3" coding agents, what this changes for engineering teams, and how to implement it without breaking your workflow.

scene_briefs.headline: Engineer Monitoring Multi-Agent Coordination
scene_briefs.scene: A developer sits at a desk with a single monitor displaying a dashboard interface showing a hierarchical task breakdown: a main orchestrator agent at the top assigning subtasks to multiple coding worker agents, each with progress bars and code snippets visible.
scene_briefs.article_grounding: Visualizes the orchestrator agent breaking down tasks and assigning them to multiple coding worker agents, illustrating the multi-agent orchestration control loop.

scene_briefs.headline: Composite Code Output from Multiple Agents
scene_briefs.scene: A close-up view of a developer’s monitor showing a code editor window where code segments from different agents are highlighted in distinct colors, stitched together seamlessly into a single coherent function or module.
scene_briefs.article_grounding: Represents how outputs from multiple coding agents are integrated into a coherent code change by the orchestrator.

scene_briefs.headline: Engineer Handling a Failure Mode in Multi-Agent Workflow
scene_briefs.scene: A developer at their desk with a monitor displaying an error notification linked to one coding agent’s output, while the orchestrator agent’s interface suggests a retry or reassignment of the subtask, showing the human in the loop managing failure recovery.
scene_briefs.article_grounding: Depicts the failure modes and tradeoffs in multi-agent orchestration and how engineers intervene to maintain workflow continuity.

scene_brief.headline: Engineer Monitoring Multi-Agent Coordination
scene_brief.scene: A developer sits at a desk with a single monitor displaying a dashboard interface showing a hierarchical task breakdown: a main orchestrator agent at the top assigning subtasks to multiple coding worker agents, each with progress bars and code snippets visible.
scene_brief.article_grounding: Visualizes the orchestrator agent breaking down tasks and assigning them to multiple coding worker agents, illustrating the multi-agent orchestration control loop.

articles.title: What AI Coding Tooling Changes for Teams Shipping With Coding Agents
articles.description: A practical breakdown of how one developer runs their day on an AI coding agent stack, and what that implies for engineering teams adopting similar workflows.

scene_briefs.headline: Developer at Web Interface Managing AI Tasks
scene_briefs.scene: A developer seated at a modern desk, focused on a widescreen monitor displaying a web-based AI coding agent interface (Codex). The desk has minimal clutter, with a notebook open showing handwritten task notes. The background includes a small whiteboard with a few sticky notes representing task priorities.
scene_briefs.article_grounding: Reflects the developer starting their day in a web interface (Codex) to manage AI coding tasks, emphasizing the workspace setup for AI agent interaction.

scene_briefs.headline: Task Planning in Project Management Tool
scene_briefs.scene: A close-up view of a developer’s desk showing a single monitor displaying the Linear project management interface. The screen highlights a list of small, focused tasks derived from user feedback. The developer’s hand is visible, using a mouse to prioritize or reorder tasks.
scene_briefs.article_grounding: Captures the moment of planning the day in Linear, turning user feedback into actionable, focused tasks as described in the article.

scene_briefs.headline: Parallel AI Coding Tasks in Progress
scene_briefs.scene: A developer at their desk with a monitor split into multiple windows, each running a different AI coding agent task in parallel. The developer is actively typing or reviewing code on one window while monitoring progress on others. The desk includes a coffee cup and a smartphone showing notifications, indicating multitasking and workflow management.
scene_briefs.article_grounding: Illustrates the workflow moment where the developer kicks off and manages 3–4 parallel tasks using AI coding agents, highlighting multitasking and AI-driven work.

scene_brief.article_grounding: Reflects the developer starting their day in a web interface (Codex) to manage AI coding tasks, emphasizing the workspace setup for AI agent interaction.
